objective: Test whether dynamic unseen-value/signature pairing can turn the Wave 62 capacity-clearing source into rpf50 pre-materialization candidate support.
non_negotiables: This is a pre-materialization diagnostic and must not be treated as selected exact support.
Rows must still require materialization, replay, leakage, and baseline gates before promotion.
The report must compare against Wave 63 and Wave 64 bounded selector results.
A partial unlock must record the remaining support gap explicitly.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a dynamic unseen-value/signature pairing preflight for the Wave 62 left-supply source.
acceptance: The report records pre-materialization selected pairs and topology counts.
The report records rejection counts before materialization.
Docs state that the report is not selected exact support.
Docs state the remaining gap to the 150-row rpf50 target.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 65 as a dynamic-pairing partial unlock that remains short of rpf50 and pre-materialization only.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that dynamic preflight reaches 137 pairs.
Docs state that rpf50 still needs 150 rows and remains 13 rows short before materialization.
Docs compare against Wave 63's 12 rows and Wave 64's 4 rows.
Docs route next work toward additional value supply or materialization-aware dynamic selection.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_65_dynamic_pairing_preflight.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 65 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 65 dynamic pairing preflight report is valid JSON.
